1132.reason: [This check is unsafe and incorrect: 1) It may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException when fewer than 3 tokens remain; 2) It doesn't properly distinguish between Decl and FuncDef according to grammar rules. According to the grammar, Decl starts with 'const', 'int', or 'char', while FuncDef starts with a FuncType ('void', 'int', 'char') followed by an identifier and '('. The current logic only checks for '(' at position 2, which fails for valid declarations and function definitions.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [This check is unsafe and logically incorrect: (1) It may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException when fewer than 3 tokens remain; (2) It assumes function definitions always have '(' at offset 2, but 'main' function has specific syntax 'int main()' and declarations might also be followed by identifiers with parentheses in complex cases; (3) It doesn't properly distinguish between Decl, FuncDef, and MainFuncDef according to grammar rules]
